stages.name: React
stages.description: Planning is spreadsheets and tribal knowledge. Demand plan is basically last year plus a gut adjustment from sales. Supply side firefights: expedites, air freight, angry calls to suppliers. No single version of the truth — finance, sales, and ops each have their own numbers.
tells: Customer can't answer "what's your forecast accuracy?" because nobody measures it

stages.name: Anticipate
stages.description: Functional planning exists but in silos. There's a demand planner running stat forecasts, a supply planner running MRP, maybe a monthly S&OP meeting that's really just a review deck.
tells: Plans exist but don't talk to each other
The S&OP meeting relitigates the past instead of making forward decisions

stages.name: Integrate
stages.description: Where IBP as a discipline (not just the product) starts. One demand signal feeds supply, inventory, and financial planning. S&OP has real scenario discussion — "if we take this promotion, what does it cost us in capacity?" Constrained planning replaces unconstrained wish lists. Most mid-to-large manufacturers claim to be here; genuinely maybe 20-30% are.
tells: One demand signal feeds supply, inventory, and financial planning
Scenario trade-offs are actually discussed in S&OP

stages.name: Collaborate / Orchestrate
stages.description: The plan extends outside the four walls — supplier collaboration, customer forecasts, clear-to-build visibility. Planning cadence tightens from monthly to weekly or continuous. Exceptions drive the work rather than full-cycle replanning. Trade-offs are made in financial terms, not just units.
tells: Supplier/customer signals are inside the plan
Exception-driven work instead of full-cycle replanning
Trade-offs argued in dollars, not units

stages.name: Autonomous / Self-correcting
stages.description: Touchless planning for stable segments, ML-driven sensing, planners manage the algorithm's exceptions rather than the plan itself. Real but rare — mostly pockets within advanced CPG and high-tech, not whole enterprises.
tells: Touchless planning exists for stable segments
Planners manage exceptions to the algorithm, not the plan
